decisions.briefing: Markets pulled back mildly today with the S&P 500 down 0.5% and NASDAQ off 0.7%, suggesting a neutral-to-cautious tone. No new buys are possible today — cash sits at $298, below the $500 threshold required to open a new position. Existing holdings look stable: NN is slightly below cost but well within the -7% stop-loss floor, and the rest of the portfolio continues to carry a strong +30% overall gain.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities slipped modestly on June 3rd, with the NASDAQ leading declines at -0.7% as tech and growth names faced light selling pressure. The S&P 500 fell 0.5%, reflecting a broad but shallow risk-off shift with no single dramatic catalyst. Traders appeared to take profits after recent gains, keeping volume subdued and direction neutral ahead of upcoming economic data.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市6月3日小幅回落，納斯達克指數跌0.7%，科技與成長類股承壓較明顯。標普500指數下跌0.5%，整體呈現溫和的風險規避情緒，並無單一明顯利空觸發。市場人士普遍認為此為近期漲勢後的獲利了結行為，成交量清淡，方向偏中性，靜待後續經濟數據指引。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag occurs when uninvested cash in a portfolio earns little to nothing while the market moves upward, reducing overall returns. Today's situation illustrates the flip side: holding cash below the $500 buy threshold means we can't act on high-momentum candidates like POET or MRX even though slots are open. Keeping just enough cash for opportunities — but not too much — is a key balancing act in active portfolio management.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指投資組合中未投入的現金幾乎不產生收益，而市場卻持續上漲，導致整體回報率下降。今日情況正好說明另一面：現金餘額不足500美元門檻，即便有空缺持倉，也無法買入POET或MRX等動能強勁的標的。如何在把握機會與保留流動性之間取得平衡，是主動管理投資組合的重要課題。
